about.quote: The system is no longer working for us, so we are charting a new course.
about.headline: Logos exists at the intersection of human exploration and technological revolution.
about.body: We are a movement for builders, explorers, and dreamers who refuse to accept the world as it is. We're part of a generation fed up with corruption, surveillance, and stagnation often associated with incumbent institutions.
about.intro: Civil society is in decline. Our institutions are failing. Our money is debased. People are atomised, indebted, and increasingly unable to provide for themselves or their families. Reform from within cannot reverse this because the system selects for adherence, and centralisation leads inevitably to corruption.
about.closing1: Logos exist to build the decentralised infrastructure and living ecosystem that makes a parallel society possible for anyone ready to reclaim their agency in the digital and physical world.
about.closing3: Built for exit when the existing order can no longer hold.

debt.title: Debt
debt.body: We were born into a debt we did not take on, for spending we did not approve, on terms we cannot refuse. Bills compound and interest accrues. The generation that took out this loan will not be the one to pay it.
debt.fact1: Global government debt has climbed to a record $111 trillion in 2025, rising by $8.3 trillion in a single year.
debt.fact2: The average millennial in the US has 30% less wealth than the average Boomer by age 35.
debt.fact3: 3.4 billion people now live in countries that spend more on interest payments than on either health or education.
debt.fact4: Just 27% of Gen Z and 55% of millennials own a home, compared to 80% of Baby Boomers.

surveillance.title: Surveillance
surveillance.body: We are the most watched generation in human history. Every click, every step, every conversation is harvested, stored, and sold without our consent. Privacy is no longer the default. It's a privilege you have to fight for.
surveillance.fact1: There are now ~1 billion surveillance cameras installed worldwide. More than 1 for every 8 people on Earth.
surveillance.fact2: 75% of police forces globally now have access to facial recognition technology.
surveillance.fact3: Governments now legally purchase massive quantities of citizens' personal data from commercial brokers.

corruption.title: Corruption
corruption.body: Policy is no longer written for the public. It is written for whoever can afford the lobbyists, the donations, and the seat at the table. This is not a conspiracy. It is the operating model.
corruption.fact1: Billionaires are now estimated to be 4,000 times more likely to hold political office than ordinary citizens.
corruption.fact2: In the US alone, federal lobbying smashed records in 2025, with corporations and interest groups spending $5.08 billion in a single year to influence Washington.
corruption.fact3: Two-thirds of Americans (67%) now believe their federal government is "corrupt", and trust in government has crashed to just 17%.

stagnation.title: Stagnation
stagnation.body: Productivity is up. Education is up. Wages are flat. The wealth is being created but it is being captured upstream, by asset holders, financial intermediaries, and the institutions closest to the money printer. The distribution is not an accident. It is the design.
stagnation.fact1: The 12 richest billionaires now own more wealth than the poorest half of humanity — 4 billion people.
stagnation.fact2: Millennials work in an economy 70% more productive than when boomers were the same age — yet their median compensation has barely moved.

builderPortal.title:
Basecamp
The local launcher
for the Logos stack
builderPortal.description:
Basecamp is a ready-to-run Logos distribution that you deploy on your own hardware, under your control. From the moment it’s installed, you’re standing on your own ground, not connecting to someone else’s infrastructure.

The Logos runtime runs locally, core modules are loaded, and essential applications — wallet, messenger, file sharing, blockchain explorer — are ready to use from day one.

paths.kicker: The right response to broken systems is not to reform them but to build alongside them, and eventually, beyond them.

paths.body: Parallel societies are communities, institutions, and economies that operate outside the legacy system and answer to the people inside them. Logos provides the infrastructure. The rest is participation.

lambdaPrize.title: Lambda Prize | Logos
lambdaPrize.description: Lambda Prize is a competitive prize framework for developers shipping privacy-preserving software on the Logos stack.

hero.label: Prize
hero.heading:
The frontier is open.
Build what's next.
hero.body: Prize is the competitive prize framework powering the Logos network. A programme to accelerate real, shipped applications built on the Logos stack.

1.body: Prizes are broken into three categories - small, medium, and large.

2.body: All prizes are listed in the GitHub repository.

3.body: Submissions are made as pull requests to the repository.

4.body: Prizes will remain open until they are claimed or explicitly closed.

5.body: Proposals are evaluated based on specific criteria outlined for each prize.

6.body: Winners will be notified directly, after which rewards will be issued.

evaluation.heading: Evaluation Criteria
evaluation.secondaryCta: Connect with the team on Discord

1.label: Step 1
1.body: Pre-Screen Checklist: Submissions are reviewed by Logos core contributors to ensure baseline criteria are met.

2.label: Step 2
2.body: Technical Evaluation: Logos core contributors will clone submission repositories to a clean environment and run a demo script without modification to verify success criteria and spot-check code quality and architecture.

1.title: Whistleblower — censorship-resistant document upload and indexing Basecamp app
1.body: This prize funds "Whistleblower", a Logos Basecamp app that lets anyone upload a document and make it permanently discoverable — without relying on any centralised server, without requiring the publisher to hold tokens, and without a single point of censorship.

2.title: Autonomous AI Module with Wallet, Storage, and Messaging
2.body: This prize is for a Logos Core module that runs an autonomous AI agent with native access to the full Logos stack: a LEZ wallet, Logos Storage, and Logos Messaging. The agent has its own identity and funds, can communicate with its owner via chat, and can communicate with other agents.

3.title: Anonymous Forum with Threshold Moderation and Membership Revocation
3.body: This prize is for a protocol for anonymous, moderated forums. Members post without revealing their identity and their posts are unlinkable to one another — yet a coordinated group of moderators can act on individual pieces of content, and a member who accumulates too many moderation strikes loses their membership.

about.heading: About the Programme
about.body: The systems that were supposed to protect us, the institutions, the corporations, the platforms, have been captured. They surveil us, extract from us, and make decisions on our behalf without our consent. We are not asking them to change. Lambda Prize is for the builders who want to create the alternative.

1.label: Real outcomes, not proposals
1.body: Prizes go to shipped code that meets defined criteria.

2.label: A stack that is private by default
2.body: Privacy is a requirement for sovereignty. Build applications that simply can't exist elsewhere.

3.label: Builders get support on their journey
3.body: Regular office hours, livestreams, and direct access to the ecosystem development engineering team to support you.

support.heading: Get Support

whoWeAre.principlesBody: Our mission is to empower individuals to achieve their aspirations and create a better future for all.  Where freedom, liberty, and human sovereignty are protected, and censorship resistance is ensured.
whoWeAre.howWeWorkBody: We build decentralised technology and inspire change. We work with local communities to use our technology to build the tools they need.

ourWork.title: Our Work
ourWork.body: We build the tools for people to create what they need. We create the underlying technology. People all over the world have created amazing applications specific to their needs.
ourWork.cardBody:
Local communities are using Logos-aligned tools to coordinate around practical needs, from neighbourhood organising to digital rights.

These case studies show how civic action and resilient technology can reinforce each other.

Each example starts with a concrete problem and a small group of people willing to build a better path.

research.title: Research | Logos
research.description: Logos Research conducts practical research across protocols, privacy, peer-to-peer systems, and civil society infrastructure.
research.heading: Logos Research

hero.kicker: Build the future of Civil Society.
hero.title:
Logos
Research
hero.description: Logos Research conducts innovative exploration within the Logos ecosystem and wider IFT. Embedded within R&D service units, we ensure the work remains practical and relevant to Logos and the IFT.

paragraphs: Logos Research, formerly Vac, is a principle-driven R&D team that provides comprehensive technical support to Logos and other IFT projects. Logos Research consists of specialised R&D service units and innovative incubator projects. We focus on developing foundational components and unopinionated protocols that both IFT projects and the broader community can utilise.

Part of our work is conducting applied research that drives the development of cutting-edge protocols, libraries, specifications, and publications. Our dual approach combines hands-on development with in-depth research, allowing us to create robust solutions tailored to the needs of the Logos ecosystem, IFT projects, and decentralised technologies in general.
Logos Research maintains an active forum and research platform, fostering collaboration and knowledge exchange within the broader community.
As custodians of the components and protocols we develop, we adhere a set of principles that guide our work, ensuring alignment with our values and objectives in advancing free and open technologies.

Logos Research conducts innovative exploration within the Logos ecosystem and wider IFT. Embedded within R&D service units, we ensure the work remains practical and relevant to Logos and the IFT. Rather than focusing on academic publications, we aim to actively bridge theory and practice. Logos Research collaborates with IFT projects to implement our research findings, and identifies and initiates new incubator projects, translating our research into real-world applications.

Logos Research spans multiple R&D service units, primarily ACZ and P2P. Key focus areas of Logos Research include zero-knowledge proofs (ZKP) and their applications, libp2p gossipsub improvements, and anonymisation networks. By maintaining close ties with various Logos and IFT teams, we drive innovation and practical advancements across the projects we work with. In the realm of ZKP, Logos Research has made contributions that have given rise to the incubator project Logos Execution Environment.

copy.contact: Get in touch with us by <discord></discord>, <forum></forum>, or <github></github>.

copy.whatBody: We are interested in both research and code contributions.

faq.title: FAQ | Logos
faq.description: Frequently asked questions about Logos — what it is, how it works, and how to get involved.
faq.heading: FAQ

1.question: What is Logos?
1.answer: Logos is a movement and a technology stack for building parallel societies — sovereign communities that govern, transact, and communicate without permission. It combines peer-to-peer networking, private messaging, censorship-resistant storage, and a programmable settlement layer into a single coherent platform.

2.question: Who is behind Logos?
2.answer: Logos is developed and supported by the Institute of Free Technology (IFT), a non-profit organisation that funds open-source projects building the infrastructure for digital sovereignty.

3.question: How is Logos different from a regular blockchain?
3.answer: Logos is a full network stack rather than a single chain. It bundles a decentralised messaging layer (Waku), a private storage layer (Codex), and a settlement layer (Nomos) so that applications can run end-to-end without trusted intermediaries.

4.question: Is Logos open source?
4.answer: Yes. Every component of the Logos stack is released under permissive open-source licences. You can read, audit, fork, and contribute to the code on GitHub.

5.question: How can I get involved?
5.answer: You can join the conversation on the Logos and Research forums, contribute code on GitHub, apply for an RFP through the Builders Hub, or start or join a Circle in your region.

6.question: What is a Circle?
6.answer: A Circle is a local, self-organising community of people who share the values of the Logos movement. Circles host events, run workshops, and support each other in adopting sovereign tools.

7.question: Does Logos have a token?
7.answer: Information on token economics will be published as the network approaches mainnet. Until then, beware of any project claiming to be an official Logos token.

8.question: Where can I follow Logos updates?
8.answer: Follow Logos on X, join the Discord, subscribe to the Blog, or watch updates on YouTube. Links are available in the footer of every page.
